title: Electric Service Connection/Repair - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Utility Assistance > Electric Service Connection/Repair 12 Matching Service Providers BLACHLY-LANE ELECTRIC COOPERATIVE PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) is an operational practice an electric utility may use to shut off power in high-risk areas to help prevent wildfires during extreme and dangerous weather conditions. PSPS means Blachly-Lane Electric Cooperative will shut off power, and it is considered an action of last resort to help protect public safety. 90680 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 688-8711 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL LINCOLN PUD PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) is an operational practice an electric utility may use to shut off power in high-risk areas to help prevent wildfires during extreme and dangerous weather conditions. PSPS means Central Lincoln PUD will shut off power, and it is considered an action of last resort to help protect public safety. 2129 N Coast Highway Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (877) 265-3211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF ASHLAND REPORT AN ISSUE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community members a service to report experiencing or witnessing a suspected city code violation such as an abandoned vehicle, graffiti, power outage or water leak, water system issue, or water quality concern, parking violation on public street, street maintenance or traffic concern, weed abatement issue, and illegal dumping. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm by appoitment only Email (541) 488-6002 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF BEND REPORT AN ISSUE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community members a service to report experiencing or witnessing a suspected city code violation with a focus on health and safety. Types of issues that can be reported but are not limited to building or land use violations, animal control, illegal dumping or excessive trash, graffiti, street maintenance, camping in the public right of way (including those in vehicles), noise complaints, abandoned vehicles, and public utility issues. 710 NW Wall Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 693-6911 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONSUMERS POWER INC CONSUMERS POWER INC LEBANON OFFICE PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) is an operational practice an electric utility may use to shut off power in high-risk areas to help prevent wildfires during extreme and dangerous weather conditions. PSPS means Consumers Power Inc (CPI) will shut off power, and it is considered an action of last resort to help protect public safety. Additionally, as a wholesale power customer of the Bonneville Power Administration (BPA), CPI may also be subject to a PSPS initiated by BPA. 1900 West Oak Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 929-3124 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONSUMERS POWER INC CONSUMERS POWER INC PHILOMATH OFFICE PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) is an operational practice an electric utility may use to shut off power in high-risk areas to help prevent wildfires during extreme and dangerous weather conditions. PSPS means Consumers Power Inc (CPI) will shut off power, and it is considered an action of last resort to help protect public safety. Additionally, as a wholesale power customer of the Bonneville Power Administration (BPA), CPI may also be subject to a PSPS initiated by BPA. 6990 West Hills Road Philomath, OR 97370 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 929-3124 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COOS-CURRY ELECTRIC COOPERATIVE PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) is an operational practice an electric utility may use to shut off power in high-risk areas to help prevent wildfires during extreme and dangerous weather conditions. Fields complaints and calls about utility emergencies. Provides a medical certificate program. May provide assistance or payment plans to customers who are having difficulty paying their utility bills.
